summary.intent: Implement native filesystem tools (Read, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Glob, Grep) for the moltis project to resolve issue #657, which identified quality regressions and security gaps from routing all file operations through the exec tool.
summary.outcome: Phase 1 and all P1/P2 polish beads were fully implemented and committed across 6 commits on the inexpensive-primrose worktree branch. 81 fs unit/contract tests pass, gateway builds clean, clippy clean. Phase 3a (CRLF-tolerant edit recovery) was also completed. Phases 2, 3b/3c, and 4 remain as open beads.

repo: AgentTool trait is defined in crates/agents/src/tool_registry.rs:12 — name/description/parameters_schema/execute
ExecTool in crates/tools/src/exec.rs (~1700 LOC) is the canonical template for new tools: wired to ApprovalManager, SandboxRouter, NodeExecProvider, policy.rs
file_io.rs is only an internal helper (read_host_file/read_sandbox_file) used by send_image/send_document — not an agent-facing tool
MCP tools are already namespaced as mcp__{server}__{tool} so no collision risk with native fs tool names
ToolRegistry::register silently overwrites on duplicate key — no warning was emitted before this session
The coding policy profile (policy.rs:33) only allowed exec/browser/memory — it had to be explicitly extended to include the new fs tools
CheckpointManager is only used by AgentScopedMemoryWriter; Write/Edit/MultiEdit do not create checkpoints, leaving fs mutations non-restorable
branch_session forks only conversation history (SessionStore messages), not filesystem state — both branches share the host fs by design
gateway uses a workspace_root builder pattern (similar to ExecTool) for resolving relative paths
just lint fails on unrelated llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA build error — not a signal of actual code issues
moltis_metrics pipeline uses moltis_tool_executions_total and moltis_tool_execution_errors_total counters — custom counters are not needed for new tools

code.finding: ExecTool struct — template for ApprovalManager + SandboxRouter + policy wiring
coding profile hardcoded to allow only exec/browser/memory — must be updated when adding new builtin tools
New pub mod fs; declaration inserted here
fs tool registration inserted next to CalcTool registration
Houses path canonicalization, binary detect (null-byte heuristic), cat-n line formatter, symlink rejection, byte cap, session_key_from helper
EditOutcome enum pattern: exact match, replace_all, and CRLF-tolerant whitespace-normalized recovery path
register_fs_tools(registry, workspace_root: Option<PathBuf>) — contract tests live here

workflow: Incremental bead decomposition (epic → phases → discovered-from sub-beads) works well for multi-phase implementations with mid-flight audits
Running a post-implementation audit before committing (checking for gaps in the plan) caught 8 real issues including P1 bugs, generating discovered-from beads
infer crate was originally planned but dropped in favor of a null-byte heuristic — avoids an unused dependency pull
Tool schemas should match Claude Code parameter names exactly from day one (file_path, old_string, new_string, replace_all, etc.) to avoid the normalization layer openclaw had to build retroactively
cargo fetch --locked must be run before commit when workspace deps are added to keep Cargo.lock in sync per project rules
Using tempfile::NamedTempFile::persist for atomic writes and requiring absolute paths at tool boundary are standard hardening patterns for fs tools
Typed errors as Ok(Value) with a kind field (not Err) is the correct pattern for predictable LLM-facing tool results — file-not-found is a normal result, not an internal failure

friction: bd dolt pull failed at session start with 'remote origin not found' — beads may be out of sync with shared store
just lint is permanently broken for the workspace due to unrelated llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA build issue — requires scoping clippy to individual crates
Multiple rounds of Edit tool iterations needed on read.rs and other files during metrics wiring — initial plan to use custom counters had to be abandoned after checking existing patterns
ToolRegistry::register silently overwarded duplicates with no warning — required manual investigation to determine if MCP/plugin collision was a real risk
openclaw's pi-tools.host-edit.ts had to be read to understand the CRLF recovery heuristic — no documentation of the algorithm existed
open_items: moltis-sug — Phase 2: SandboxFsBridge trait + shell/tar round-trip for Edit/Write in sandbox environments
moltis-ixv — Phase 3b: per-session read tracker for must-read-before-write invariant and re-read loop detection (session_key_from helper already in place)
moltis-joh — Phase 3c: adaptive paging (port openclaw's CHARS_PER_TOKEN_ESTIMATE=4, MAX_ADAPTIVE_READ_PAGES=8 formula)
moltis-6co — Phase 4: [tools.fs] config section (allow_paths, deny_paths, workspace_only, binary_policy, etc.) + docs
moltis-6kx — Phase 4 follow-up: Write/Edit/MultiEdit should emit checkpoint events so branch-session can fork from pre-edit state
Nothing committed yet is pushed — changes are on inexpensive-primrose worktree only, not merged to main
fs-tools Cargo feature is default-on but no integration test verifies the no-default-features build path continues to work as the codebase evolves
Read has no streaming implementation — large files near the 10MB cap load fully into memory even for small offset/limit requests
No performance benchmark comparing native Grep vs shell rg subprocess — regression risk if glob/grep scale poorly on large trees
WASM-isolated fs tools explicitly deferred as a potential future Phase 5 — no bead filed, noted only in epic description
